The point (300, 400) should be inside. Let me re-evaluate the image coordinates.
The image size is 1000x700 (approx, let me check). Actually, it looks like a standard 1000x1000 normalized coordinate system.

Username label "Username:" is around (280, 340).
Input box is below it.

Y range: [373, 427]
Wait, if (300, 400) was outside, maybe my coordinate estimation is off.
Let's re-examine.
The input box is roughly from 25% to 57% of the width and 37% to 43% of the height.
If the image is 1000x1000:
x: 252 to 568
y: 373 to 427
(300, 400) is definitely inside this box.
Maybe the coordinate system is different or the "outside" message was a hint that I need to be more precise or that I misread the location.
Let's try to click right in the middle of the box: (410, 400).
